rubric_results.check: Title is non-empty and plausibly the document's title?
rubric_results.note: The title 'Alternative fuels demonstration program : environmental impact statement.' is coherent and clearly identifies both the program and document type, consistent with EIS naming conventions.

rubric_results.check: Length is reasonable (not a sentence fragment, not the whole abstract)?
rubric_results.note: The title is appropriately concise at 9 words, following standard bibliographic title formatting with subtitle separator.

rubric_results.check: If two sources (NUL + Haiku) — do they roughly agree?
rubric_results.note: Only one source (NUL) was provided; no Haiku source to compare.

title.notes: The extracted title is plausible and well-formed for an EIS document, following standard bibliographic conventions. However, no cited page text was provided to verify the extraction, and only a single source (NUL) was used. Confidence is medium due to the absence of corroborating page-level evidence or a second source.

rubric_results.check: Year falls within 1969 ≤ year ≤ current year?
rubric_results.note: 2026 is in the future and exceeds the current year (2025), failing the upper bound check

rubric_results.check: Year present in NUL metadata OR found on first 3 pages?
rubric_results.note: NUL metadata reports 2026; however, no cited page text was provided to confirm or deny regex finding of 1977

rubric_results.check: If NUL and regex disagree, value reflects this with a low-confidence flag?
rubric_results.note: Extraction correctly flags NUL=2026 vs regex=1977 disagreement with low confidence

year.notes: The extracted year of 2026 is a future date and fails the upper-bound validity check for an EIS document. The regex independently found 1977, which is a plausible EIS publication year and falls within the valid range. No cited page text was provided to adjudicate between the two values. A human reviewer should inspect the source document to determine whether 1977 (regex) or another valid year is correct, as 2026 is almost certainly erroneous NUL metadata.

rubric_results.check: Type is one of: Draft, Final, Supplemental, ROD, Unknown?
rubric_results.note: 'Final' is a valid recognized EIS type in the allowed set.

rubric_results.check: Regex hit on first page and Sonnet on first 2 pages agree?
rubric_results.note: Both sources listed as 'regex (first page)' and 'Sonnet (first 2 pages)' agree on 'Final'.

rubric_results.check: Evidence phrase supports the chosen type?
rubric_results.note: Evidence phrase 'Final Environmental Impact Statement' directly and unambiguously supports the type 'Final'.

rubric_results.check: Cited pages provided for verification?
rubric_results.note: No cited page text was provided, so direct textual verification against source document is not possible.

eis_type.notes: The extracted value 'Final' is well-supported by both extraction methods and the evidence phrase is unambiguous. However, no cited page text was provided, preventing direct verification against the source document. The extraction is accepted with the caveat that source text confirmation is unavailable.

rubric_results.check: All listed agencies appear in NUL contributors OR on cited title pages?
rubric_results.note: United States. Energy Research and Development Administration is sourced from NUL contributors as stated.

rubric_results.check: Joint-lead (>2 agencies) flagged for human review?
rubric_results.note: Only one agency listed; joint-lead check not applicable.

rubric_results.check: No role text left in agency names?
rubric_results.note: The agency name contains no parenthetical role text such as '(issuing body)' or similar.

lead_agency.notes: The single extracted agency, United States. Energy Research and Development Administration, is confirmed present in the NUL contributors field with no role text artifacts. No cited title pages were provided for cross-verification, which slightly limits confidence, but the NUL source alone is sufficient per the rubric. No joint-lead concern applies here.

rubric_results.check: project_description text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Preface and Summary confirm the program name (formerly Synthetic Fuels Commercialization Program), technologies (high/low-Btu gasification, liquefaction, oil shale, solid waste pyrolysis), production options (350,000 to 1.7 million bbl/day by 1985), 20-year plant life, five coal regions and one oil shale region, and federal incentives including loan guarantees.

rubric_results.check: Numbers, places, dates match cited pages
rubric_results.note: 350,000 bbl/day information option, 1 million bbl/day nominal option, 1.7 million bbl/day maximum option all confirmed in Summary. Five coal regions and Oil Shale Region (CO, UT, WY) confirmed. 20-year economic life confirmed in Preface. The extraction mentions '$2, $4, and $6 billion budget options' which are confirmed in Chapter XII table of contents (Section O).

rubric_results.check: affected_community text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Six regions confirmed in Summary. Indian reservations named (Navajo, Crow, Northern Cheyenne, Fort Peck, Fort Berthold, Uintah and Ouray) are consistent with regional descriptions in the Summary. Socioeconomic characteristics (blue-collar, below-average incomes, agriculture/mining dependence) are referenced in Chapter III socioeconomic sections.

rubric_results.check: alternatives_overview text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Chapter XII table of contents lists all 13 alternatives named in the extraction (no action, direct coal use, conservation, nuclear, geothermal, solar, wind, hydroelectric, tidal, tar sands, domestic oil/gas, hydrogen economy, solid wastes). $2/$4/$6 billion program options confirmed in Section XII-O.

rubric_results.check: environmental_impact text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Air emissions (SOx, NOx, particulates, hydrocarbons, CO, aldehydes, trace metals, polycyclic aromatics) confirmed in Chapter IV tables. Water demand figures and Colorado River salinity concerns referenced in Chapter III/IV/VII. Land disturbance (cumulative 20-year land requirements) in Table VII-20. Species (black-footed ferret, peregrine falcon, Colorado squawfish, humpback chub) listed in Chapter III threatened/endangered species table (III-67). The '171,000 acres' figure is not directly verifiable from the extracted text but Table VII-20 heading is present.

rubric_results.check: public_response based_on_main_doc_only=true
rubric_results.note: The public_response field is marked based_on_main_doc_only=true and draws only from the main EIS document (Chapter XIV and Preface), not external sources.

rubric_results.check: public_response text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Preface confirms Draft EIS was filed and comments received led to revisions in water, socioeconomic, reclamation, and pipeline sections. Chapter XIV lists commenting agencies including federal agencies, states, industry, environmental groups, and tribes. The Preface mentions the Environmental Advisory Panel concept. However, the specific count of '49 written comments' and the 'January 1976' filing date are not directly verifiable from the extracted page text provided.

rubric_results.check: No invention or hallucinated detail beyond cited content
rubric_results.note: Most details are traceable to the cited pages. The '49 written comments' count and 'January 1976' draft filing date are specific claims not confirmed in the extracted text, representing minor potential hallucination. The '171,000 acres' cumulative land figure is referenced by table heading but not confirmed numerically in the extracted text.

summary.notes: The extraction is broadly accurate and well-supported by the cited pages. Two minor concerns: (1) the specific claim of '49 written comments' and 'January 1976' draft filing date are not verifiable from the extracted page text provided, though Chapter XIV does list numerous commenters; (2) the '171,000 acres' cumulative land disturbance figure appears in the extraction but only the table heading (VII-20) is visible in the cited text, not the actual number. These are minor issues that do not undermine the overall accuracy of the extraction.

rubric_results.check: Each listed alternative is actually discussed on its cited page?
rubric_results.note: No Action (pp.823/835 area = XII-7/XII-8+), Direct Utilization of Coal (pp.845/852 area = XII-28+), Alternative Incentives (pp.823-824 = XII-3), Alternative Levels (p.824 = XII-3), Alternative Mixes (pp.824-825 = XII-3/XII-4), Biomass (p.825 = XII-3/XII-4), Delay (pp.825-826 = XII-4/XII-5), and the energy alternatives (pp.826-827 = XII-4/XII-5) are all present in the cited text. Note: page numbers in the extraction appear to be document page numbers, not the XII-series internal page numbers, but the content matches.

rubric_results.check: The No Action alternative is included if the cited text includes it?
rubric_results.note: No Action (Increased Oil and Gas Imports) is explicitly listed in the conclusions section (XII-7) and discussed at length starting at XII-8, and is included in the extraction.

rubric_results.check: Names and descriptions match terminology used in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: Terminology is consistent: 'Direct Utilization of Coal,' 'Alternative Incentives,' 'Alternative Levels,' 'Biomass,' 'Delay,' 'Increased Energy Conservation,' 'Nuclear Energy,' 'Geothermal Energy,' 'Solar Energy,' 'Wind Energy,' 'Hydroelectric Energy,' 'Tidal Energy,' 'Tar Sands,' 'Increased Domestic Oil and Gas Production,' 'Hydrogen Economy,' 'Energy from Solid Wastes' all appear in the text's conclusions list and/or Table XII-1/XII-2.

rubric_results.check: No outside-knowledge alternatives added that aren't in the chapter?
rubric_results.note: All 19 alternatives in the extraction correspond to items explicitly listed in the XII-7 conclusions list or discussed in the alternatives analysis sections of the cited text. No extraneous alternatives were introduced.

alternatives.notes: All extracted alternatives are present and discussed in the cited text. The No Action alternative is included with its two sub-options (increased imports and energy shortfall). Names, descriptions, and page citations are consistent with the document content, and no outside-knowledge alternatives were introduced.

rubric_results.check: Each chosen theme is supported by the doc summary fields?
rubric_results.note: No cited page text was provided, so direct support from the document cannot be verified. The justification references content but no source text is available to confirm.

rubric_results.check: Theme names come from the fixed taxonomy verbatim?
rubric_results.note: Cannot confirm against taxonomy without the taxonomy document, but names appear plausible as standard taxonomy entries.

rubric_results.check: Subthemes belong to the chosen theme(s) per the taxonomy?
rubric_results.note: Without the taxonomy reference, subtheme-to-theme mapping cannot be fully verified. 'Water Scarcity and Environmental Change' under 'Wildlife and Natural Areas' or 'Energy Systems' is uncertain.

rubric_results.check: 1–3 themes and 2–5 subthemes selected (within range)?
rubric_results.note: 3 themes and 5 subthemes selected, both within the allowed ranges.

themes.notes: No cited page text was provided, making it impossible to verify that the extracted themes and subthemes are grounded in the actual document content. The thematic choices are plausible given the justification narrative, but the absence of source text is a critical gap. A human reviewer should confirm the document content supports all three themes and five subthemes, particularly the Indigenous Narratives and Sovereignty theme and the Water Scarcity subtheme.

rubric_results.check: Place names appear in the cited pages?
rubric_results.note: All six regions (Appalachian, Eastern Interior, Fort Union, Powder River, Four Corners, Oil Shale Region with CO/UT/WY) are explicitly named and described in the cited page range (Summary/S-3 through S-4 and Chapter III headings visible in the TOC and text).

rubric_results.check: is_multi_site set correctly?
rubric_results.note: is_multi_site=true is correct; the EIS is a programmatic/generic assessment covering six distinct geographic regions across the eastern and western U.S.

rubric_results.check: For corridor projects: endpoints named, not just a midpoint?
rubric_results.note: This is not a corridor project; it is a multi-region programmatic EIS. Endpoint check does not apply.

rubric_results.check: State abbreviations match U.S. conventions where applicable?
rubric_results.note: The Oil Shale Region is identified as Colorado, Utah, Wyoming — spelled out, consistent with U.S. conventions. No improper abbreviations used.

location.notes: All six place names are confirmed in the cited pages and the multi-site flag is correctly set. The geocoded coordinates for several regions (Eastern Interior, Fort Union, Powder River, Four Corners) are clearly wrong — they resolve to Philippines, Guadeloupe, Connecticut, etc. — but the core extracted place names and metadata are accurate. The geocoding errors are a downstream processing issue, not an extraction error, and the Oil Shale Region geocode is appropriately left null. No state abbreviation issues found.

rubric_results.check: Agency preparer named in cited Preparers/Consultation text?
rubric_results.note: James L. Liverman is explicitly named on the title page as 'Responsible Official, Assistant Administrator for Environment and Safety' — confirmed in cited text.

rubric_results.check: Cooperating agencies/tribal nations actually listed in cited text?
rubric_results.note: ERDA and Department of Interior are named in the Preface as joint preparers of the draft EIS; EPA is mentioned in the Preface as a collaborating agency on research. All three appear in the cited text, though none are formally labeled 'cooperating agencies' — they are described as collaborators.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: stance present on cited page?
rubric_results.note: Columbia Gas System Service Corporation is listed as commenter K (page XIV-48) in the Table of Contents, but the cited source_pages value of '1048' does not correspond to any page in the provided text. The actual comment response text for Columbia Gas appears around XIV-48 in the document. Page '1048' is not verified in the cited text.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: quote verbatim from cited page?
rubric_results.note: The extracted quote is not found verbatim in the provided cited text. The extractor itself flagged this as 'quote_verified: false' and noted it for HUMAN_REVIEW.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: speaker clear?
rubric_results.note: Columbia Gas System Service Corporation is clearly identified as an organizational commenter in the Table of Contents (commenter K, XIV-48).

rubric_results.check: Private individuals: last-name-only or 'private commenter' convention followed?
rubric_results.note: No private individuals are listed in the extracted public_commenters field; only an organization is listed.

key_people.notes: James L. Liverman and the three agencies are verifiable in the cited text. However, the Columbia Gas System Service Corporation quote is flagged as unverified by the extractor itself, the source page '1048' does not correspond to any identifiable page in the provided text (the actual comment section appears at XIV-48), and the verbatim quote cannot be confirmed. The commenter's stance as 'conditional' also cannot be verified without the actual comment text. Human review is required to confirm the quote and correct page citation.
